vehicle.label: Tiltrotor VTOL Hybrid
vehicle.desc: Fixed-wing VTOL with 2 tilting rotor nacelles, BVLOS long-range capable

parts.label: Center Fuselage Spine
parts.spec: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

parts.label: Main Wing (2-piece)
parts.spec: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

parts.label: Nacelle Assembly x 2
parts.spec: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

parts.label: Tilt Servo + Linkage x 2
parts.spec: 25kg-cm DS3218 servo per nacelle, titanium pushrod, dual ball joints, tilt range 0-93deg, crash-tolerant

parts.label: V-Tail x 2 (Ruddervator)
parts.spec: +-35deg V-tail ruddervator pair, MG996R servo each, 30deg dihedral, CFRP sandwich panel, hinge piano wire

parts.label: Landing Gear (Tri)
parts.spec: Tricycle landing gear, spring-steel skid arms, 55mm dia wheels, CG at 1/3 chord, 200mm wheelbase

parts.label: Battery Bay (Slide-In)
parts.spec: Belly slide-in battery bay, 6S 8000mAh Li-ion pack, Velcro + strap dual retention, CG position 28% MAC

parts.label: Payload Nose Bay
parts.spec: Interchangeable nose payload bay, Day/IR camera gimbal or survey sensor, 80x60x50mm, 300g payload limit

parts.label: Tilt-Axis Harness + Datum Service Bay
parts.spec: Tiltrotor integration_service module with tilt-axis harness_channel, slack_loop, interface_datum, service_hatch, connector keying, and nacelle-to-wing load-path inspection access

risk.desc: Potential thermal expansion issues due to material selection.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: CFRP box beam center fuselage, avionics bay, wing spar socket, front GPS boom mount, belly camera window

physics_paths.description: Nacelle loads route through spar_socket into fuselage spine
Cooling air flow through avionics bay vents
Heat dissipation from avionics bay to the outside
Nacelle tilt motion transmitted to fuselage spine via bearing seats
Structural pressure from flight maneuvers
Electrical and data connections from avionics bay to external modules
Service access for maintenance and inspection of avionics bay

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces and rail guides
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and tactile inspection for fit and finish

verification_focus.description: Ensure fuselage spine can handle nacelle loads
Verify heat dissipation from avionics bay
Check electrical connections from avionics bay
Confirm correct assembly sequence and order

risk.desc: Potential salt-fog corrosion on CFRP components.
risk.mit: Apply corrosion-resistant coatings to exposed CFRP surfaces.

risk.desc: Nacelle tilt mechanism clearance may be insufficient for long-term operation.
risk.mit: Increase clearance between nacelle and wing spar socket.

risk.desc: Service access for nacelle motor/servo replacement may be obstructed.
risk.mit: Ensure service access panels are easily removable and unobstructed.

risk.desc: Thermal dissipation may be inadequate in high-temperature environments.
risk.mit: Increase thermal dissipation features such as heat sinks and cooling channels.

risk.desc: Electrical harness routing may be prone to damage during assembly.
risk.mit: Protect harnesses with flexible conduits and ensure proper routing.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Straight wing, 4S NACA 2412, dual 15mm CFRP spar, 900mm half-span, 160mm chord, 3-layer CFRP skin

physics_paths.path: Nacelle loads from rotor thrust to fuselage spine via wing spar and spar socket
Airflow over wing surface for lift generation
Heat dissipation from wing structure to ambient air
Nacelle tilt mechanism motion through wing spar socket and bearing seats
Atmospheric pressure on wing surface for lift generation
Nacelle motor/servo control signals and power through harness passthroughs
Service access for nacelle motor/servo replacement through access covers

integration_opportunities.integration_opportunity: Wing spar socket alignment and bolted connection

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary locating faces, rail guides, axis rings, spar sockets, or mating pads
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: H7/h6 fit class for bolted interfaces, LC2 for snap-fit interfaces
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures for FDM parts, orientation for minimal support
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Visual and dimensional inspection of interfaces and critical features

verification_focus.focus: Wing spar load capacity and nacelle load routing
Nacelle tilt mechanism clearance and bearing seat fit
Service access for nacelle motor/servo replacement

risk.desc: Risk of thermal stress due to high temperature differential between motor and cooling fins.
risk.mit: Implement additional cooling measures or redesign cooling fins for better heat dissipation.

risk.desc: Risk of mechanical failure due to insufficient preload on critical joints.
risk.mit: Increase preload torque or add locking mechanisms to critical joints.

risk.desc: Risk of assembly errors due to ambiguous assembly sequence instructions.
risk.mit: Clarify assembly sequence with detailed step-by-step instructions and tool requirements.

risk.desc: Risk of improper alignment due to lack of alignment features.
risk.mit: Add alignment features such as pins or dowels to ensure precise alignment.

risk.desc: Risk of service access issues due to limited access to motor and ESC.
risk.mit: Design larger access covers or additional access points for easier serviceability.

_subsystem_plan.subsystem_role: Tilting nacelle assembly, 0-90deg tilt axis, 6309 bearing set, servo-driven worm gear, motor and ESC pocket

physics_paths.description: Rotor thrust load is transferred through the bearing seat into the wing structure.
Tilt axis allows the nacelle to rotate from 0 to 90 degrees for VTOL transition.
Motor heat is dissipated through cooling fins to ambient air.
ESC powers the servo through electrical connections.
Motor and ESC can be accessed and serviced through an access cover.

adjacent_interfaces.assembly_order_hint: Pre-assemble wing spar socket before mounting nacelle
Align fuselage spine with nacelle mounting flange before snap fitting
Route harness through nacelle access cover before connecting to payload

internal_feature_targets.description: Axis around which the nacelle tilts
Connects the servo to the tilt mechanism
Enhances heat dissipation from the motor
Allows access to motor and ESC for service

integration_opportunities.description: Integrate wing spar socket for structural integrity
Integrate harness passthrough for electrical connections
Integrate cooling fins for motor heat dissipation

manufacturing_strategy.datum: Primary datum is a flat surface on the bottom of the nacelle
manufacturing_strategy.tolerance: Tolerances are H7/h6 for bolted interfaces and LC2 for snap-fit interfaces
manufacturing_strategy.support: Support structures are oriented for easy removal during post-processing
manufacturing_strategy.inspection: Inspection points include critical joints and service access points

risk.mit: Apply corrosion-resistant coating to pushrod

Include service access panel for maintenance.

risk.desc: Potential thermal stress due to sun exposure on CFRP panel.
risk.mit: Implement thermal management strategies such as heat sinks or insulation.

risk.desc: Salt-fog resistance may degrade over time, affecting structural integrity.
risk.mit: Use salt-fog resistant coatings and materials.

risk.desc: High vibration loads from rotor may cause fatigue in piano wire hinges.
risk.mit: Design hinges with higher fatigue resistance and regular inspection intervals.

risk.desc: Limited clearance for ruddervator motion may restrict control effectiveness.
risk.mit: Increase clearance between ruddervator and surrounding structure.

risk.desc: Potential for water ingress through unsealed interfaces.
risk.mit: Ensure all interfaces are properly sealed and waterproofed.
